title: Core execution model
description: Define the explicit execution model for isolated rehearsal runs, step semantics, and cleanup behavior.
acceptance_criteria: - RunContext, StepRunner, and CleanupManager boundaries are defined\n- failure semantics are explicit\n- teardown behavior is modeled before convenience helpers
close_reason: RunContext, StepRunner, and CleanupManager are now defined with explicit tests and failure semantics, completing the core execution model epic.

title: Introduce RunContext
description: Centralize run identity, workdir structure, derived paths, metadata, and artifact preservation under an explicit RunContext type.
acceptance_criteria: - run_id generation is centralized\n- workdir and artifact paths are derived from RunContext\n- state is passed explicitly instead of being reconstructed ad hoc
notes: Starting RunContext implementation: explicit run identity, derived paths, and unit tests.
close_reason: RunContext now centralizes run id generation, derived filesystem layout, metadata materialization, and preserved artifact paths with unit coverage.

title: Introduce StepRunner
description: Standardize named step execution, process output handling, logging, and failure propagation.
acceptance_criteria: - steps have stable names\n- stdout/stderr capture is consistent\n- exit handling is uniform across steps
notes: Starting StepRunner implementation: explicit named step execution, output capture, and uniform failure semantics.
close_reason: StepRunner now standardizes named command execution, stdout/stderr capture, event emission, spawn failure handling, and non-zero exit propagation with unit coverage.

title: Introduce CleanupManager
description: Define cleanup registration, reverse-order execution, failure cleanup, explicit teardown, and recovery hints.
acceptance_criteria: - cleanup actions can be registered explicitly\n- reverse-order cleanup semantics are defined\n- failure cleanup preserves artifacts and emits recovery hints
notes: Starting CleanupManager implementation: explicit cleanup registration, reverse-order execution, artifact preservation, and recovery hints.
close_reason: CleanupManager now supports explicit cleanup registration, reverse-order teardown, failure artifact preservation, recovery hints for failed actions, and cleanup failure reporting with unit coverage.

title: Refactoring and stabilization
description: Remove procedural leftovers and stabilize the codebase after the first happy path exists.
acceptance_criteria: - duplicated orchestration paths are removed\n- implicit state flow is eliminated\n- isolation guarantees are validated

title: Remove procedural orchestration leftovers
description: Eliminate duplicated or bypassed control flow after the first end-to-end path exists.
acceptance_criteria: - duplicated orchestration code is removed\n- implicit dependencies between steps are reduced\n- the happy path remains intact

title: Eliminate global mutable state
description: Push run state and configuration through explicit context instead of ambient process state where practical.
acceptance_criteria: - state flows through RunContext or explicit inputs\n- hidden environment coupling is reduced\n- tests can construct execution state directly

title: Validate isolation guarantees
description: Check that runs do not interfere with one another and that backend isolation semantics are explicit.
acceptance_criteria: - backend state isolation is validated\n- run-local artifacts are separated cleanly\n- interference risks are documented or addressed

title: Deployment backend abstraction
description: Define how deployment engines plug into dress without leaking tool-specific assumptions across the whole codebase.
acceptance_criteria: - DeploymentBackend contract is explicit\n- Terraform/OpenTofu path has an obvious place to fit\n- future CloudFormation support remains possible without premature abstraction
close_reason: The deployment backend abstraction is now defined, and the first Terraform/OpenTofu backend skeleton fits behind it without leaking backend-specific assumptions into the core.

title: Define DeploymentBackend
description: Define the minimal contract for deployment engines without leaking Terraform/OpenTofu behavior into the whole system.
acceptance_criteria: - interface covers initialize, deploy, outputs, and destroy\n- backend-specific details stay behind the boundary\n- CloudFormation remains conceptually possible
notes: Starting DeploymentBackend implementation: explicit backend contract for apply, outputs, and teardown without concrete backend logic yet.
close_reason: DeploymentBackend is now defined as a narrow contract with generic request/session types, normalized outputs, and backend-agnostic error handling.

title: Add Terraform/OpenTofu backend skeleton
description: Create the first backend implementation skeleton around Terraform/OpenTofu using the DeploymentBackend contract.
acceptance_criteria: - module exists for Terraform/OpenTofu backend\n- isolated backend configuration has a defined place\n- no full execution flow is implemented yet
notes: Starting Terraform/OpenTofu backend skeleton: concrete backend type, config shape, and command-building surface without full execution flow.
close_reason: Terraform/OpenTofu now has a concrete backend skeleton with isolated config, command builders, and a DeploymentBackend implementation without a full scenario flow yet.

title: First AWS happy-path rehearsal
description: Move one real AWS happy path through the new abstractions without widening scope prematurely.
acceptance_criteria: - one AWS scenario runs through deploy, verify, and teardown\n- the happy path uses the new abstractions rather than bypassing them\n- observable workflow remains understandable

title: Implement the first AWS happy path
description: Move one AWS ECS Express HTTP rehearsal through RunContext, StepRunner, DeploymentBackend, Scenario, VerificationSpec, and CleanupManager.
acceptance_criteria: - one real happy path reaches deploy, verify, and teardown\n- failure cleanup preserves artifacts\n- the flow uses the new abstractions rather than procedural shortcuts

title: Preserve observable workflow quality
description: Ensure the first happy path keeps logs, outputs, and operator behavior understandable while using the new abstractions.
acceptance_criteria: - step naming remains clear\n- artifact locations are understandable\n- failure reporting remains actionable

title: Project scaffolding and architecture
description: Establish the repository shape, architectural boundaries, and implementation guardrails for the dress infrastructure rehearsal engine.
acceptance_criteria: - repository scaffold exists with clear module boundaries\n- README and AGENTS communicate intent and guardrails\n- architecture documents the core concepts without overcommitting implementation details
close_reason: Phase-1 scaffold and guardrail work is complete: the project skeleton exists and the initial architecture is documented.

title: Create the project skeleton
description: Materialize the repository layout, Rust crate shape, and top-level docs so future work starts from clear ownership boundaries.
acceptance_criteria:
- src/, tests/, and docs/ directories exist
- crate exposes structural modules for the main architectural concepts
- CLI binary is named dress
- no real orchestration logic is implemented yet
close_reason: Repository scaffold now includes the crate layout, CLI binary, docs, and tests directory expected by the phase-1 skeleton task.

title: Document the initial architecture and guardrails
description: Capture the intent, boundaries, non-goals, and engineering guardrails before implementation begins.
acceptance_criteria: - README explains purpose and metaphor succinctly\n- AGENTS encodes implementation guardrails\n- docs describe RunContext, StepRunner, CleanupManager, DeploymentBackend, Scenario, and VerificationSpec
close_reason: README, AGENTS, and architecture docs now capture the initial purpose, guardrails, and core concepts.

title: Scenario abstraction
description: Define how target-specific rehearsal behavior plugs into the common execution model.
acceptance_criteria: - Scenario contract is explicit\n- target-specific setup, discovery, and cleanup expectations are separated from orchestration\n- scenarios are not modeled as script chains

title: Define Scenario contract
description: Define how target-specific preparation, discovery, verification wiring, and cleanup expectations plug into the harness.
acceptance_criteria: - Scenario is not modeled as script chaining\n- setup, output discovery, and cleanup expectations have explicit hooks\n- orchestration does not own target-specific assumptions

title: Sketch AWS ECS Express scenario boundary
description: Identify the target-specific responsibilities for an AWS ECS Express HTTP rehearsal without implementing the full scenario.
acceptance_criteria: - AWS-specific responsibilities are enumerated at the Scenario boundary\n- bootstrap, endpoint discovery, and prerequisite concerns are located outside orchestration\n- no generic machinery is introduced beyond current AWS needs

title: Design validation
description: Pressure-test the architecture for coupling, cleanup guarantees, and remaining hard-coded assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: - change-isolation checks exist\n- cleanup guarantees are exercised\n- remaining shortcuts are documented

title: Validate change isolation
description: Verify that modifying verification or bootstrap behavior does not unintentionally reshape deployment or cleanup boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: - verification changes can be reasoned about independently of deployment\n- bootstrap changes do not silently alter cleanup semantics\n- coupling risks are documented

title: Validate cleanup guarantees
description: Simulate mid-run failure and teardown paths to confirm cleanup remains deterministic and understandable.
acceptance_criteria: - failure-triggered cleanup runs in reverse order\n- artifact preservation on failure is validated\n- recovery hints remain actionable

title: Document remaining hard-coded areas
description: Record any remaining shortcuts or intentionally narrow assumptions after the first flow exists.
acceptance_criteria: - known limitations are documented\n- each shortcut has a justification\n- future extraction points are clear

title: Verification model
description: Define how dress expresses readiness, verification requests, assertions, retries, and failure evidence.
acceptance_criteria: - VerificationSpec boundary is explicit\n- success criteria are testable\n- failure artifact capture has a defined place

title: Define VerificationSpec
description: Define readiness targets, request shapes, assertions, retries, and failure artifact capture as an explicit verification model.
acceptance_criteria: - verification inputs are modeled explicitly\n- retry and timeout policy have a defined place\n- failure evidence capture is part of the contract

title: Sketch HTTP verification flow
description: Define the first verification path for HTTP scenarios using VerificationSpec without prematurely generalizing.
acceptance_criteria: - HTTP request shape and assertions are represented explicitly\n- verification integrates conceptually with StepRunner and CleanupManager\n- the shape remains narrow
